existing_Entity_Description: New Westminster is a municipality in Metro Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada, within the Metro Vancouver. As of the 2021 Canadian Census, New Westminster had a population of 78,916. The community is situated in a vibrant, world-class metropolitan area where mountains meet the Pacific Ocean. one of Canada's most competitive markets with premium real estate values. The region features mild coastal climate with rainy winters and warm, dry summers.

REGION_BUNDLE.climate: Mild oceanic coastal climate: wet, mild winters (rain far more often than snow at sea level), warm dry summers; heavy November-March rainfall drives moisture, drainage and moss/algae issues on building exteriors; freeze-thaw is rare at sea level but real on the North Shore slopes.
REGION_BUNDLE.terrain: Delta and floodplain of the Fraser River meeting the Coast Mountains and the Salish Sea; North Shore municipalities climb steep forested mountain slopes; Richmond and Delta sit on low-lying river delta soils.
REGION_BUNDLE.economy: Canada's third-largest metropolitan economy: Port of Vancouver (Canada's largest port), film/TV production, tech, finance, construction and real estate; major institutions include UBC, SFU, Vancouver International Airport (YVR) in Richmond.
REGION_BUNDLE.building_stock: The widest mix in BC: pre-war character homes and Vancouver Specials, leaky-condo-era mid-rises now rehabilitated, glass high-rise towers, and large new master-planned suburbs (Surrey, Langley); strata-governed multi-family housing is a defining feature.
